zh.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Claxton是Hammond Claxton和Alice Cockett的女兒，出生在英國英格蘭素福爾可迪斯頓。她和Lord Mayor John Marsham至少有5個兒子和3個女兒。她去世於1569年8月4日之前在英國諾福爾可諾維其，埋葬在英國英格蘭諾福爾可諾維其。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Claxton是Hammond Claxton和Alice Cockett的女儿，出生在英国英格兰素福爾可迪斯頓。她和Lord Mayor John Marsham至少有5个儿子和3个女儿。她去世于1569年8月4日之前在英国諾福爾可諾維其，埋葬在英国英格兰諾福爾可諾維其。

nameMeanings.name: Claxton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of various places called Claxton, for example in County Durham, Norfolk, and Yorkshire, or from Long Clawson (Leicestershire). All are derived from an English genitive form of the Old Norse personal name Klakkr (see Clack ) + Old English tūn ‘settlement’.

variants.name: Clack
meanings: from the Old English personal name Clacc or its Old Norse (Danish) cognate Klak. As a personal name this is from a word meaning ‘lump’ and may have been used as a nickname for a large or thickset man.
possibly sometimes from a derivative of Middle English clakken ‘to rattle, clatter’, denoting a chatterer, alternatively, perhaps a miller, from the clack or clatter of his mill, or a bellringer.

variants.name: Cluxton
meanings: English: variant of   Claxton  . This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Clawson
meanings: Americanized form of North German, Danish, and Norwegian Clausen , Claussen , or Klausen , and also of their Dutch cognates, such as Claassen or Klaassen, patronymics from short forms of the local equivalents of the personal name Nicholas . Compare Clauson .

variants.name: Claxon
meanings: English (Essex): variant of Claxton . This name is absent from the medieval record, and is therefore unlikely to be a patronymic from the medieval personal name Clak + son.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Claxton was born in Chediston, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom as the daughter of Hammond Claxton and Alice Cockett. She had at least 5 sons and 3 daughters with Lord Mayor John Marsham. She died before 4 August 1569, in Norwich, Norfolk, England, and was buried in Norwich, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Claxton est née à Chediston, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, fille de Hammond Claxton et Alice Cockett. Elle a eu au moins 5 fils et 3 filles avec Lord Mayor John Marsham. Elle est décédée avant 4 août 1569, à Norwich, Norfolk, Angleterre, et a été enterrée à Norwich, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Claxton wurde in Chediston, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom, als Tochter von Hammond Claxton und Alice Cockett geboren. Sie hatte mit Lord Mayor John Marsham mindestens 5 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb vor 4. August 1569 in Norwich, Norfolk, England und wurde in Norwich, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Claxton è nata il nel Chediston, Suffolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito come figlia di Hammond Claxton e Alice Cockett. Lei avuto almeno 5 figli e 3 figlie con Lord Mayor John Marsham. Morì prima del 4 agosto 1569, nel Norwich, Norfolk, England, e fu sepolta a Norwich, Norfolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth ClaxtonはイギリスイギリススフォルクチェディストンでHammond ClaxtonとAlice Cockettの娘として生まれた。彼女はLord Mayor John Marshamとの間に少なくとも5人の息子と3人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1569年8月4日以前にイギリスノーフォルクノーウィチで死去し，イギリスイギリスノーフォルクノーウィチにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Claxton(은)는 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 체디스턴에서 Hammond Claxton(와)과 Alice Cockett의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 Lord Mayor John Marsham(와)과 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1569년 8월 4일 이전 영국 노르폴크 노위치에서 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 노위치에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Claxton nasceu em Chediston, Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom filha de Hammond Claxton e Alice Cockett. Ela teve pelo menos 5 filhos e 3 filhas com Lord Mayor John Marsham. Ela faleceu antes 4 de agosto de 1569, em Norwich, Norfolk, Inglaterra, e foi sepultada em Norwich, Norfolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Claxton родилась в местности чедистон, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания. Её родителей звали Hammond Claxton и Alice Cockett. У неё было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 3 дочери от Lord Mayor John Marsham. Она умерла перед 4 августа 1569 г. в местности норвич, норфолк, Англия и была похоронена в местности норвич, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Claxton nació en Chediston, Suffolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido como hija de Hammond Claxton y Alice Cockett. Tuvo por lo menos 5 hijos y 3 hijas con Lord Mayor John Marsham. Murió antes de 4 de agosto de 1569, en Norwich, Norfolk, Inglaterra, y fue sepultada en Norwich, Norfolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
